reasoning_model_1: Model 1 fails to strictly adhere to the content requirements. At timestamp [0.00s-0.50s], it adds a "Shh" sound which was not present in the requested text "快走，有人来了". Regarding the core feature "whisper" (耳语/Volume), while the volume is reduced, the speech from [0.50s-2.00s] is delivered as 'quiet voiced speech' (breathy phonation with discernible pitch) rather than a true unvoiced whisper. The vocal cords are clearly vibrating, which deviates from the definition of a whisper. The addition of extra content and the failure to fully unvoice the speech lowers the score significantly.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 demonstrates excellent adherence to both content and paralinguistic requirements. The text matches the script exactly with no additions or omissions [0.00s-1.80s]. Crucially, the model successfully generates a true unvoiced whisper. There is no fundamental frequency/pitch detected in the speech, relying entirely on turbulent airflow to form speech sounds, which perfectly aligns with the command "耳语" (whisper). The pacing implies urgency effectively, making it a highly natural and accurate generation.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 committed a content error by adding an unprompted "Shh" sound effect. Furthermore, Model 1 failed the core paralinguistic challenge; it merely lowered its volume and used a breathy tone but remained voiced (speaking quietly), whereas Model 2 achieved a technically accurate unvoiced whisper. Model 2 represents a 'Fully correct' execution, while Model 1 has major issues with both content accuracy and feature definition.
